🎯 Agentic Issue Triage\n\nThis is a sub-issue of [#53864 – Azure Mgmt Generator migration](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864), tracking the **MPG (Management Plane Generator) migration for the Azure Maps management SDK** from Swagger/AutoRest to TypeSpec-based generation.\n\n\u003e ⚠️ The issue body is currently empty — details about scope, progress, or blockers have not yet been filled in.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📋 Context \u0026 Background\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- The parent tracking issue [#53864](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/53864) describes a broad effort to migrate all Azure management-plane SDKs to the new TypeSpec/mgmt-generator pipeline.\n- The Maps SDK lives under `sdk/maps/` and contains `Azure.ResourceManager.Maps` (and possibly data-plane packages).\n- Completed RP migrations are tracked in [#55080](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55080).\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e✅ Suggested Sub-Tasks Checklist\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [ ] Identify the TypeSpec spec location for Azure Maps (`specification/maps/` in `azure-rest-api-specs`)\n- [ ] Verify `tsp-location.yaml` exists or create it under `sdk/maps/Azure.ResourceManager.Maps/`\n- [ ] Run `azsdk_package_generate_code` (or `Generate.ps1`) to regenerate from TypeSpec\n- [ ] Fix any breaking changes (use `CodeGenSuppress`, `CodeGenMember`, partial classes, `@@clientName` as needed)\n- [ ] Run `Export-API.ps1` to update API listings\n- [ ] Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test` — resolve any failures\n- [ ] Run pre-commit checks (`dotnet format`, snippet updates)\n- [ ] Open a PR and ensure CI is green\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e🔧 Debugging / Migration Tips\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- Use the `mpg-sdk-migration` skill for guided migration steps.\n- Use the `mitigate-breaking-changes` skill for SDK-side customization patterns (`CodeGenType`, `CodeGenSuppress`, `@@clientName`, `hierarchyBuilding`).\n- If you encounter `CS0103 'scope does not exist'` errors in generated sync methods, this is a known generator bug — check the mgmt emitter for the `using DiagnosticScope scope = ...` line in sync method bodies.\n- If `@@clientName` on union types causes broken `global::.TypeName` in `ModelFactory`, use `CodeGenSuppress` + a custom method.\n- Enable `enable-wire-path-attribute: true` in `tspconfig.yaml` if the existing package used `WirePathAttribute`.\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e📚 Relevant Resources\u003c/summary\u003e\n\n- [AGENTS.md – Azure SDK for .NET](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/AGENTS.md)\n- [MPG SDK Migration skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mpg-sdk-migration/)\n- [Mitigate Breaking Changes skill](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/mitigate-breaking-changes/)\n- [Local SDK Workflow
